B1_deployed_status.conclusion: Deployed m-fiber uniqueness is outside the free-0/free-1 regimes. Entropy (log2 avg ≈ -18820) still predicts M_m^{max} is typically 0 or 1, but the only unconditional packing we prove for general free is the weak anticode / p^{free} envelope, which does not fit the atom. B1 at deployed remains OPEN as a uniqueness theorem for free=846161.
B1_deployed_status.name: deployed_B1_status
B1_deployed_status.what_would_close_B1: Any proof that M_m^{max} <= 2^{53} (even much larger than 1) combines with a U_res bound; M_m^{max} <= 1 is the entropy-natural target.

B1_free0.deployed_note: Deployed free = m-w = 846161 >> 0, so free-0 does not apply.
B1_free0.name: Mm_unique_when_w_ge_m
proof: A monic degree-m polynomial has exactly m non-leading coefficients. Specifying w >= m of the high monic coefficients (the full coefficient vector when w=m, and overdetermined when w>m) determines at most one monic polynomial.
A monic polynomial has at most one set of m distinct roots. Hence at most one m-subset lies in any depth-w fiber.
B1_free0.statement: If w >= m, then every depth-w monic-prefix fiber of m-subsets has size <= 1. In particular M_m^{max} <= 1.

B1_free1.name: Mm_pack_when_w_eq_m_minus_1
proof: Monic degree m with the top m-1 monic coefficients fixed: only the constant term remains free. That is a constant-shift pencil Lambda_c = X^m + a_1 X^{m-1} + ... + a_{m-1} X + c.

If two distinct members both split completely over D into distinct roots, their root sets are disjoint: a shared root x would force Lambda_c(x)=Lambda_{c'}(x)=0 hence c=c'. Pack m-sets into a ground set of size n: at most floor(n/m) members.
B1_free1.statement: If w = m-1 (exactly one free lower coefficient), then the m-subsets sharing a fixed depth-w monic prefix form a constant-shift family of monic locators, and M_m^{max} <= floor(n/m).

B1_side_free1.name: side_e_subset_always_free1
proof: Apply free-1 packing with m replaced by e=w+1: free = e-w = 1 automatically.
B1_side_free1.statement: For e = w+1, every depth-w monic-prefix fiber of e-subsets has size <= floor(n/e). (This is free = e-w = 1.)

B2_U_res_structure.name: U_res_structural_bounds
B2_U_res_structure.open: Prove an absolute upper bound U_res(z) <= target/17 without assuming |R| small. Circular bound U_res <= |R| does not close the atom alone.
proof: (i) Each residual S contributes one lex side and one u; map S |-> u has image size U_res and domain size |R|.

B2_partial_global.deployed_note: p^w is ~2^{2.09e6}, far above the atom. The bound is structural, not finite-useful without a residual sparsity constraint on which pencils appear.
B2_partial_global.name: U_res_le_num_nonempty_side_fibers_global
proof: By definition of U_res as a set of realized prefixes.
Each nonempty e-fiber is one constant-shift pencil (free-1); distinct pencils have distinct length-w prefixes (the free constant does not affect the prefix).
Hence nonempty e-prefixes inject into the set of such pencils, size <= C(n,e) and <= p^w.
B2_partial_global.statement: U_res(z) is at most the number of depth-w monic prefixes u for which the e-subset fiber is nonempty AND at least one U in that fiber is the lex-min e-set of some S in R(z). In particular U_res(z) <= |{u : e-fiber(u) nonempty}| <= p^w, and more tightly U_res(z) <= C(n,e) with the free-1 refinement that the nonempty u are in bijection with constant-shift pencils that hit at least one residual lex side.

combined_criterion.name: atom_from_B1_and_B2
proof: |R| <= pack * N_can_prim <= pack * K * U_res <= target.
combined_criterion.statement: If M_m^{max} <= K and max_z U_res(z) <= floor(target_floor / (pack_ceil * K)) for a positive integer K, then max |R(z)| <= target_floor.

title: B1 free-regime uniqueness and B2 U_res structure
